news.title: Teamwork with “Tiki-Tapa”: The Secret to Spain’s World Cup Successes
news.lede: I am not an attentive soccer (football) follower, but it was hard to ignore the 2026 FIFA World Cup being played in my own country (or at least my own continent). I tuned in for more and more games as the play progressed. Spain progressed further and further along, and finally won the gold. This was not a one-off fluke.

news.title: China’s World Cup frenzy: 70 billion views, 83.3 billion yuan in lottery football sales
news.lede: The Fifa World Cup really grabbed the attention of football fans and gamblers in China, according to data from the state broadcaster and sports lottery. CCTV, the state broadcaster, shared that its tournament broadcasts, special programming and news coverage reached a cumulative cross-media audience of more than 70 billion individual views by the eve of the World Cup final. During the final between Argentina and Spain, the broadcaster revealed that live cross-media broadcasts reached a cumulative audience of 282 million, peaking with a real-time viewership of 41.23 million. It was not just casual football fans who enjoyed the World Cup – gamblers also watched with heightened tension as China’s sports lottery market experienced a surge in sales during the tournament.

George Mills: "I'd never wish ill on someone but there was a part of me almost relieved to see Rodri's struggles last season after returning from the ACL rupture that kept him sidelined for most of 2024-25.

news.title: Blame FIFA, not the USA, as $14bn 64-team World Cup angers football purists in losing battle
news.lede: That's been the case for decades and the overwhelming success of the biggest World Cup in history will only embolden Gianni Infantino in his global football push. With the USA (and Canada and Mexico) pulling off a 104-match tournament that featured 48 teams bouncing across three countries, FIFA could fast track a push for further World Cup expansion in time for the next tourney in 2030. It was initially expected that Infantino and Co. would at least hold off until a 2038 World Cup that is still waiting for a host country. "In 2030, the World Cup comes to Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay, a great opportunity for football, to celebrate the World Cup Centenary with a 64-team tournament," CONMEBOL president Alejandro Domínguez posted on social media.

news.title: College football’s best QBs for 2026, plus poll results
news.lede: The Pulse Newsletter 📣 | This is The Athletic’s daily sports newsletter. Don’t spy on anyone today. Coming up: 🏈 CFB QB tiers 📺 Clark out at ESPN 🗳️ Your poll results As one football leaves us, the next one settles in right on time. The World Cup is in the past now.

news.title: Only four years to the next men’s World Cup – what can we expect from the 2030 edition?
news.lede: If you thought the 2026 tournament was sprawling, think again with six host countries over three continents getting involved in four years’ time. If the 2026 World Cup was the tournament’s largest edition yet – featuring 48 teams for the first time in its 96-year history, being played in three different countries – the 2030 World Cup is going to have an even bigger footprint. The tournament is set to be jointly hosted by Morocco, Portugal and Spain, marking the first time the World Cup will be held in more than one confederation and the first time it will visit north Africa. In addition, though, and in honour of the World Cup’s 100-year anniversary, a special match is scheduled to be held at Estadio Centenario in Montevideo, Uruguay – the stadium that hosted the 1930 World Cup final.

news.title: World Cup 2026 in 90 seconds: a photo animation with audio
news.lede: Picture editor Jonny Weeks has created a rapid audio-visual review of the tournament featuring key moments, colourful fans ... and the precious trophy. This video contains flashing images and an audio track. Top 30: from half a million World Cup photos, why I love these the most.
